MatrixSourcesMethodChangesReports through 2025

Place-based evidence, not an impact score

Where canopy, vulnerability, and shared work meet.

The public Greensboro Canopy assessment adds a reproducible 2012-2022 baseline, modeled ecosystem benefits, and tract-level planting priorities to the annual-report story.

See the decade changeOpen the City resource hub

Assessment snapshot

A richer baseline than a single milestone.

Values are modeled assessment estimates. They are not audited financial savings, annual SEP outcomes, or a count of trees planted by a particular partner.

2012 to 2022

See where canopy grew and where it dropped off.

The source compares two imagery years. That supports a ten-year change story by land-use stratum, but not an annual trend line.

Planting priority

Pair need with place, without inventing project attribution.

Explore the tracts with the largest assessed very-high and high priority acreage. Vulnerability fields inform the source model, but this view does not label a tract or resident with a project impact score.

TractVery highHighPriority shareVulnerability score

What it adds

A common evidence layer for departments and partners.

OSR can use the assessment to coordinate grant oversight and engagement; Planning can connect it to land-use strategy; the City Arborist can apply technical context; Housing & Neighborhood Development and community partners can connect priority areas to neighborhood delivery.

See the documented working relationship
Attribution boundary

The data shows shared geography and a reason to coordinate. It does not prove which organization caused observed canopy gain, loss, or benefits.

Inspect the evidence

Download, cross-reference, and reproduce.

Retrieved July 20, 2026 from public City of Greensboro ArcGIS services. No resident names or street addresses are included.

Citation tiers, used identically on every page
①SEP '22 Cited to the 2022 Strategic Energy Plan itself.
②Progress '25 Cited to the 2025 (Year 3) Annual Progress Report.
③Verified Independently sourced (Legistar, press, etc.), confirmed against full primary-source text.
④Unconfirmed Independently sourced but resting on a search-result summary only — primary text could not be fetched. Treat as provisional.
Raw data
data/index.json data/departments.json data/funding.json data/funding-linkage.json data/collaborations.json data/canopy-assessment.json data/SCHEMA.md (full schema docs)

This tool is fully re-derivable from the linked JSON — every figure traces to a citation, every gap is marked rather than omitted.
